title: Snyk pull returns zero findings + creates duplicate epic
description: kbs snyk pull run from Virtuus returns 0 findings even when Snyk shows 4 projects, and re-creates the Snyk Vulnerabilities epic each time instead of updating existing.
creator: derek.norrbom

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text: Starting investigation. Hypothesis: running from Virtuus repo uses Virtuus .kanbus.yml, creating epic in Virtuus project. Need to verify kbs snyk pull expects to run from Kanbus repo root and/or config needs org/project filters.

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text:
Findings so far:
- In rust/src/snyk_sync.rs, projects are filtered by repo prefix  where repo is auto-detected from git origin unless snyk.repo is set in .kanbus.yml. If no Snyk project name starts with that prefix, project_map is empty and pull returns 0.
- In Virtuus .kanbus.yml, snyk.repo is unset, so auto-detect is used. If your Snyk project names don’t start with the GitHub slug (e.g. Org/Repo:...), everything gets filtered out.
Next: confirm actual Snyk project names and set snyk.repo accordingly (or adjust code to make repo filter optional/fallback).

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text:
Findings so far:
- In rust/src/snyk_sync.rs, projects are filtered by repo prefix "{repo}:" where repo is auto-detected from git origin unless snyk.repo is set in .kanbus.yml. If no Snyk project name starts with that prefix, project_map is empty and pull returns 0.
- In Virtuus .kanbus.yml, snyk.repo is unset, so auto-detect is used. If your Snyk project names don't start with the GitHub slug (e.g. Org/Repo:...), everything gets filtered out.
Next: confirm actual Snyk project names and set snyk.repo accordingly (or adjust code to make repo filter optional/fallback).

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text:
Debug results: Snyk API shows 4 projects with name prefix "AnthusAI/Virtuus" and 16 medium issues across them. Kanbus filter required names starting with "{repo}:" so it excluded the base project named exactly "AnthusAI/Virtuus".
Fix: allow exact name match or name starting with "{repo}:" in both Rust and Python snyk_sync. This should make repo filter include the base project and pull the 16 issues.

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text:
Root cause: Snyk REST projects API sometimes returns an empty string for attributes.target_file on the base project ("AnthusAI/Virtuus"). Rust code treated empty target_file as valid, so it never fell back to name and excluded the base project, leaving only 3 subprojects with 0 issues. Python used `or name`, so it worked.
Fix: in rust/src/snyk_sync.rs, treat empty target_file as missing and fall back to name. Added optional KANBUS_SNYK_DEBUG logs to print counts and sample IDs when needed. After rebuild, dry-run pulls 16 medium issues.

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text: Further debug: Snyk REST /issues endpoint only returns type=package_vulnerability (dependency vulns). No code issues present in that feed, so current sync cannot pull Snyk Code findings. Explains generic descriptions + empty package names for code issues. Need to add Snyk Code endpoint support/mapping.

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text: Implemented: Snyk sync now fetches both package_vulnerability and code types, with graceful handling if code fetch is unauthorized. Bumped Snyk REST API version to 2025-11-05. Added mapping for code issues (title, file, line/col, classes, description) and custom fields. Rust + Python updated.

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text: Added Snyk Code source location parsing for sourceLocation.region (start/end line+col) and class IDs (e.g. CWE-23). Code issues now include file + precise line range and store custom fields: snyk_file, snyk_line/column, snyk_end_line/column. Python parity updated.

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text: Added local snippet extraction for Snyk Code issues using file path + start/end region. Snippets include line numbers and are added to description. Custom fields include start/end line+column. Rust + Python updated.

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text: Fix: Snyk pull now reuses existing "Snyk Vulnerabilities" epic (title + label match) when parent_epic is unset, picking most recently updated. This prevents duplicate empty epics. Rust + Python updated.

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text: Change: kbs snyk pull now creates/uses an Initiative ("Snyk Vulnerability Remediation") with two epics (Dependency/Code). Issues are grouped by type and tasks keyed by (category, target_file) with custom field snyk_category. Prevents mixing code/deps and improves LLM clarity. Rust + Python updated.

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text: Change: epic priority now derived from most severe child issue per category. Existing epics get priority updated on pull. Rust + Python updated.

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text: Created structured tasks under initiative tskl-q4h to cover the Snyk pull work: tskl-q4h.4 (Code support/mapping), tskl-q4h.5 (snippets/line ranges), tskl-q4h.6 (hierarchy + epic priority + empty epic suppression).

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Closing: tracked in the wrong project.
